mission.title: flux: flux bootstrap github - Scopes
mission.description: flux bootstrap github - Scopes. Requested by 6+ users.

steps.title: Check current flux deployment
steps.description:
Verify your flux version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
helm list -n flux 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working flux installation.

steps.title: Review flux configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant flux configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
kubectl get configmap -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=flux
```
The documentation doesn't mention which scopes are needed for the Github PAT.  Is it possible to add this information?

steps.title: Apply the fix for flux bootstrap github - Scopes
steps.description:
In the Getting Started guide, https://fluxcd.io/docs/get-started/#before-you-begin

> **A GitHub personal access token with repo permissions**. See the GitHub documentation on creating a personal access token.

To be specific, even if you intend to use Flux in read-only mode, the repo permissions for the PAT should be for full read/write. This is because Flux writes to the repo, in order to set up SSH Deploy Keys. You can decide at bootstrap time whether the permissions for the Deploy Key

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
kubectl get events -n flux --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "flux bootstrap github - Scopes" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
In the Getting Started guide, https://fluxcd.io/docs/get-started/#before-you-begin

> **A GitHub personal access token with repo permissions**. See the GitHub documentation on creating a personal access token.

To be specific, even if you intend to use Flux in read-only mode, the repo permissions for the PAT should be for full read/write.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with flux installed or the issue environment reproducible.
